name: Lee Wiley
abstract: Lee Wiley (October 9, 1908 – December 11, 1975) was an American jazz singer during the 1930s, 1940s, and 1950s.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: she takes more after the Ella Fitzgerald or Lee Wiley type of singing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Sutton
about_quotes.evidence: Lee Wiley, I remember I played behind her at the Village Vanguard with Max Kaminsky and Arthur Trappier
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Sutton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Conrad "Connie" Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And on two of the sides were Lee Wiley. And one song she sang was The Man I Love. And I think the other one was Someone To Watch Over Me. And Bobby backed her up on it, as only Bobby could do.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: He once did a set of notes that I just finally got again on Lee Wiley that was the only writing I’ve ever seen that really captured the sensuousness of which she was capable.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: he did have — there are certain things that he enjoyed and they were great jazz things, like having people like Lee Wiley as one of the singers
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Carter, we were doing “Chrysler Presents,” the singer Wiley? ... Yeah, “The Lee Wiley Story.” Benny did the music and I played the tenor
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999
